quality.rationale: 多步张量缩并验证正交性+结合矢量模长分类讨论+涉及相对论观察者分解物理背景

origin.question: Problem 6.6. Show that a contraction of a vector $\mathbf{V}$ with the “projection tensor” $\mathbf{P} \equiv \mathbf{g} + \mathbf{u} \otimes \mathbf{u}$ projects $\mathbf{V}$ into the 3-surface orthogonal to the 4-velocity vector $\mathbf{u}$ . If $\mathbf{n}$ is a unit spacelike vector show that$$\mathbf {P} \equiv \mathbf {g} - \mathbf {n} \otimes \mathbf {n}$$is the corresponding projection operator. Show that there is no unique projection operator orthogonal to a null vector.
origin.answer: 1. 缩并结果满足 $\mathbf{u} \cdot (\mathbf{P} \cdot \mathbf{V}) = 0$，且对已正交向量保持不变；2. 当 $\mathbf{n}$ 为单位类空矢量时，$\mathbf{P} = \mathbf{g} - \mathbf{n} \otimes \mathbf{n}$ 为对应投影算符；3. 对类光矢量 $\mathbf{k}$，因 $\mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{k} = 0$，正交投影算符不唯一。
origin.solution: Solution 6.6. Let $\mathbf{A}$ be an arbitrary vector. We want to see if $\mathbf{P} \cdot \mathbf{A}$ is orthogonal to $\mathbf{u}$ , i.e. if $\mathbf{u} \cdot \mathbf{P} \cdot \mathbf{A} = 0$ :$$\mathbf {u} \cdot \mathbf {P} \cdot \mathbf {A} = \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} (\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} + \mathrm {u}_{\alpha} \mathrm {u}_{\beta}) \mathbf {A}^{\beta} = \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {A}_{\alpha} + (\mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}_{\alpha}) \mathrm {u}_{\beta} \mathbf {A}^{\beta} = \mathrm {u}_{\alpha} \mathbf {A}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {u}_{\beta} \mathbf {A}^{\beta} = 0.$$Also it is easy to see that if $\mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{u} = 0$ , then $\mathbf{P} \cdot \mathbf{A} = \mathbf{A}$ , so $\mathbf{A}$ is unaffected by projection. If $\mathbf{n}$ is a unit spacelike vector, we have$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {n} \cdot \mathbf {P} \cdot \mathbf {A} = \mathfrak {n}^{\alpha} \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} - \mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \mathrm {n}_{\beta}\right) \mathbf {A}^{\beta} \\ = \mathrm {n}^{\alpha} \mathrm {A}_{\alpha} - \left(\mathrm {n}^{\alpha} \mathrm {n}_{\alpha}\right) \mathrm {n}_{\beta} \mathrm {A}^{\beta} \\ = \mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \mathrm {A}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {n}_{\beta} \mathrm {A}^{\beta} = 0. \\ \end{array}$$Likewise a vector already orthogonal to $\mathbf{n}$ is unaffected by projection. Suppose now that $\mathbf{P}$ is a projection operator orthogonal to a null vector $\mathbf{k}$ , so that $\mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{P} \cdot \mathbf{A} = 0$ for all $\mathbf{A}$ . It is easy to see that $\mathbf{P} + \text{constant} \times \mathbf{k} \otimes \mathbf{k}$ is also a projection operator since $\mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{k} = 0$ ; so $\mathbf{P}$ is not unique. (The set of null projection operators is not empty since it is easy tocheck that for any 4-vector $\mathbf{w}$ , one is $\mathbf{P} = \mathbf{g} - \mathbf{w} \otimes \mathbf{k} / \mathbf{k} \cdot \mathbf{w}$ . However, there are no symmetric null projection operators at all.)
